title: Google Remy, Grok 5, Mythos 1, New Atlas Robot, ASI… and More AI News This Month!
summary: AI Revolution reviews major AI developments including Google Remy, Grok 5, Anthropic Mythos 1, Boston Dynamics Atlas upgrades, and DeepSeek price cuts. The channel argues that the month's biggest trend is the rapid rise of AI agents capable of working, coding, perceiving, and adapting beyond traditional question-answering systems.
writeup: A monthly AI roundup covering the convergence of agentic systems across every major lab: Google's internal Remy agent (a continuous, ecosystem-integrated personal agent), Gemini 3.2 Flash improvements in SVG and 3D code generation, multi-token prediction (MTP) via speculative decoding for up to 3x inference speedup in Gemma 4, GPT-5.5 Instant's 52.5% reduction in hallucinated claims, and Anthropic's upcoming Orbit proactive briefing tool for Claude. The through-line is that agents are no longer one-off assistants responding to prompts but persistent systems managing background workflows across Gmail, Calendar, GitHub, and Slack.
key_points: Google's internal Remy agent operates continuously across Gmail, Docs, Calendar, Drive, and Search, proactively managing workflows rather than waiting for prompts, positioning it as a competitor to OpenClaw-style autonomous task agents.

Gemini 3.2 Flash, tested on the Aluther AI arena against live competitors, shows upgraded SVG generation precision, complex 3D environment code generation, improved animation processing, and better real-time responsiveness.

Google's multi-token prediction (MTP) drafters for Gemma 4 use speculative decoding: a small drafter model predicts several tokens ahead, the main model verifies them in one pass, yielding up to 3x faster inference with no quality loss and shared KV-cache to avoid redundant attention computation.

GPT-5.5 Instant, now the default ChatGPT model for hundreds of millions of users, reduces hallucinated claims by 52.5% and inaccurate claims on difficult conversations by 37.3% versus GPT-5.3 Instant, with particular gains in medicine, law, and finance.

Anthropic's Orbit, appearing as a staged settings toggle in Claude web and mobile builds, is a proactive briefing layer that pulls from Gmail, Slack, GitHub, Calendar, Drive, and Figma to surface workflow summaries without waiting for a user query.
